shared_properties: Project core palette keeps primary #535353, background #F7F7F7, and accent #FF0000.
Canvas target stays 600x150px with pixel-perfect presentation at 60 FPS.
All painted edges keep a 2px outline rhythm and flat shading.
differences: This is the narrowest ground hazard and becomes the base silhouette for larger cactus groupings.
The body stays fully static with no animation frames.
The top spike cluster stays inside a 34x70px frame.

context.usage: Baseline jump obstacle used from the opening score range onward.

shape.silhouette: Single narrow cactus stalk with two arm spikes and a flat grounded base.

acceptance_criteria: The lowest opaque pixel row forms one continuous grounded base.
Only #535353 and transparency appear in painted pixels.

reference_images.label: t_rex_runner_sprite_sheet_2x
reference_images.purpose: Grounds cactus and pterodactyl hit-read silhouettes used in obstacle timing.

reference_images.label: t_rex_runner_sprite_sheet_1x
reference_images.purpose: Grounds cactus and pterodactyl hit-read silhouettes used in obstacle timing.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle with a single nearest-neighbor texture.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer in the Loading state and store the handle in a typed resource before gameplay starts.
runtime_notes: Snap transforms to whole pixels before render extraction.
Use visibility toggles or spawn pools instead of mutating image handles during active play.
Keep collider setup separate from the art bundle so gameplay forgiveness margins remain tunable.
